comment_content: I feel like this season. I’m gonna have beef with Alex.
reply_content:
@oyin3231 He did all of that in the first half of the season — and honestly, I liked him then too. But after that? He completely switched up.

As soon as he started dating Jackie, his whole attitude changed. He got jealous, petty, and only acted flirty or bold when Cole was around, like everything became a competition. It didn’t feel genuine anymore. And yeah, I get he was hurt, but that doesn’t excuse how immature and aggressive he started acting.

Let’s not forget the mess he caused later in the season:
	•	He straight-up fought Cole over things that could’ve been handled with a conversation.
	•	He constantly gave dirty looks or made snarky comments whenever Cole was near.
	•	He started being petty and territorial, even when it wasn’t about him.
	•	He acted like he was above it all, but kept instigating drama behind the scenes.
	•	He made it uncomfortable for everyone by turning small moments into tension-filled scenes.
	•	He let his jealousy run everything — instead of being chill, he started acting entitled and angry.
	•	And let’s be real — he played the victim way too much, even though he caused a lot of the issues himself.

So yeah, early-season Alex? Cool. But late-season Alex? He was messy, aggressive, and honestly exhausting to watch. Just because he was nice at first doesn’t cancel out how he acted later. Also, just to add — Cole was already going to fix the teacup before she even told him the full story behind it. That wasn’t just something sweet he did because she asked — it showed how much he paid attention to her, how he noticed what mattered to her, and how much he genuinely cared. That was real.

And the deep conversations they had? You could feel the connection. It wasn’t just surface-level flirting — they actually saw each other and opened up in ways no one else could. That kind of emotional depth doesn’t come around easily.

Also, about the wedding — just to clear it up — Alex didn’t get drunk after Jackie didn’t say “I love you.” He got drunk before the wedding even started, because he was nervous or maybe already spiraling. Either way, it wasn’t the mature or respectful thing to do, and it just made things worse. You can’t put that all on her.

So again, thank you for saying people are allowed to want Cole as endgame — seriously, I appreciate that. I just respectfully disagree with almost everything you said 😅 but you’re absolutely allowed to have your opinion! We’re all watching the same show, just through different lenses I guess.

comment_content: I feel like this season. I’m gonna have beef with Alex.
reply_content:
@lakeshow-vq1rm I hear your perspective, but I actually disagree with a lot of what you’re saying.

I think it’s important to stop painting Alex like he’s some flawless victim in this situation. Yes, he might have opened up emotionally, but that doesn’t erase the fact that he also made some really questionable choices. People keep saying Jackie played him, but no one wants to talk about how Alex was constantly trying to one-up Cole and used his feelings for Jackie as part of that. That’s manipulation, not love. He wasn’t some saint — he just hid his intentions better.

And as for Cole, yeah, he’s made mistakes, but he was clearly hurting too. It’s like people are forgetting he went through his own emotional rollercoaster. He wasn’t perfect, but he was never pretending to be. Alex, on the other hand, put on this “good guy” mask but was just as messy underneath.

Also, not to generalize, but I know guys like Alex — they start off sweet and vulnerable, and people fall for that. But once they feel rejected or insecure, they flip. They become passive-aggressive, controlling, or emotionally manipulative. I feel like that’s exactly what happened here. Jackie probably felt that shift too, and yeah, maybe she handled it wrong, but let’s not act like Alex didn’t contribute to the mess.
This whole situation is complicated, and Alex is not innocent.
